# frameloom — Copilot Instructions

## What this repo is

`frameloom` is a **public npm package** published at https://www.npmjs.com/package/frameloom.

End users install it with `npm install frameloom` / `pnpm add frameloom` / `yarn add frameloom` and import from `frameloom/react`. Every change to the public API, behavior, or configuration has **real downstream impact**.

## Non-negotiables for every change

- **Update `CHANGELOG.md`** — every PR or commit that touches exported API, behavior, defaults, or fix must get an entry under `## [Unreleased]` following the Keep a Changelog format.
- **Update `README.md`** — if a new feature, prop, or method is added, document it in the relevant README section with a working code example.
- Never break existing public types or remove exported symbols without a major version bump.
- Never add required props to existing components — always optional with a backward-compatible default.

## Architecture

```
src/
  core/          Framework-agnostic canvas engine (no React imports)
    stage.ts     Multi-layer one-canvas renderer; normalized placement math lives here
    frameLoader.ts  URL-array frame loading with concurrency, lifecycle, abort
    assetCache.ts   Shared URL image cache (acquire/release)
    canvasRenderer.ts  DPR-aware canvas sizing + cover/contain/fill drawing
    nativeDriver.ts    Built-in rAF animation driver
    types.ts     All shared public types
  react/
    FrameSequence.tsx       Single-sequence React component
    FrameStage.tsx          Multi-layer canvas stage component
    SequenceLayer.tsx       Child layer component for FrameStage
    FrameStageContext.tsx   Child registration context (internal)
    useFrameSequenceControls.ts  Hook-first API for FrameSequence
    useFrameStageControls.ts     Hook-first API for FrameStage
    gsap.ts    Optional GSAP animation driver adapter
    motion.ts  Optional Motion/Framer Motion adapter
```

## API conventions

- React API is **hook-first**: users call `useFrameSequenceControls()` / `useFrameStageControls()`, attach the returned `.ref`, then call methods on the returned object. The component still accepts a forwarded ref directly for library integrations.
- `placement` values for `SequenceLayer` are **normalized `0–1`** relative to the parent canvas — not pixels or percentages.
- `rotation`, `skewX`, `skewY` are in **radians**.
- Animation adapters (GSAP, Motion) are peer dependencies and **completely optional** — core renders without them.

## Build and validation

```bash
npm run typecheck   # tsc --noEmit
npm run lint        # eslint
npm run build       # tsup — outputs dist/
npm pack --dry-run  # verify what gets published
```

Run all four before publishing.

## Release flow

1. Add CHANGELOG entry under `## [Unreleased]`.
2. Run validation above.
3. Commit all changes.
4. `npm version patch|minor|major` — creates version commit + git tag.
5. `npm publish --otp=<code>` — publishes to npm (2FA required).
6. Verify: `npm view frameloom version`.

## Changelog entry format

```markdown
## [Unreleased]

### Added
- `setLayerOpacity(id, opacity)` on `FrameStageControls` — sets layer alpha without re-render.

### Changed
- `placement.anchorX/anchorY` now default to `0.5` (center) instead of `0` (top-left).

### Fixed
- Fixed frame index out-of-bounds when `images` array changes length mid-playback.
```

Use **Added / Changed / Deprecated / Removed / Fixed / Security**.

## Do not

- Do not modify `dist/` directly — it is generated by `npm run build`.
- Do not add `docs/` planning files — internal notes go in session state, not the repo.
- Do not add `*.tgz` artifacts to the repo — `.gitignore` covers them.
- Do not import React inside `src/core/` — it must stay framework-agnostic.
